comments.text: It's ambiguous from the reference whether the authors intend to exclude the prime $n$ from the problem. If they do not, then this is basically a weaker version of Legendre's conjecture. If they do, then this is more or less a question about the distribution of semiprimes $pq$ with $p,q = x^{1/2} \log^{O(1)} x$ in intervals of length $O(x^{1/2} \log^2 x)$.
